search_queries_used: site:dha.gov.ae seborrheic dermatitis telehealth guideline PDF
rejection_reasons: Patient pages are not clinician guidance.
Medicine selection and doses were outside documentation scope.
Commercial product guidance is not authoritative.
population_applicability: Partial: exact for guideline-described infant, adolescent, and adult seborrheic dermatitis; every scalp, facial, fold, infectious, psoriatic, fungal, or immune-related condition is not fully covered.
setting_applicability: Direct for DHA telehealth history, appearance, red-flag, differential, and clinician-led management documentation; diagnosis, treatment, and referral remain clinician-led.
UAE_applicability: Verified for Dubai Health Authority telehealth services.
recency_verification: The current DHA Issue 2 seborrheic-dermatitis PDF, due for revision 2029-02-21, was reviewed on 2026-07-13.
superseded_check: No newer official DHA seborrheic-dermatitis issue was identified.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports seborrheic-dermatitis and dandruff terminology, scalp or facial itch and scale, greasy plaques, erythema, distribution, course, and clinician-reviewed history.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports seborrheic-dermatitis and dandruff terminology, scalp or facial scale, pruritus, greasy plaques, erythema, scalp, eyebrow, nasolabial, eyelid, ear, trunk, fold, relapsing, stress, and seasonal context.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports fever or systemic illness, painful worsening lesions, spreading infection, and clinician-documented worsening or return fields.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports fever or systemic illness, painful rapidly worsening eczema, recurrent or spreading infection, and clinician-documented worsening or return fields.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports clinician-reviewed family history and scalp or prior-treatment context without inferring a differential.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports clinician-reviewed family history in psoriasis context and KOH or fungal-culture documentation when tinea corporis is a differential, without inferring either diagnosis.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports clinician-documented skin care, dandruff shampoo, topical-treatment decisions, and follow-up without medicine names or doses.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports documenting skin-care, dandruff-shampoo, eyelid-care, clinician-stated topical treatment, and follow-up discussion without mapping medicine names, doses, frequency, duration, or autonomous advice.

unresolved_source_gaps: Mucosal involvement, immunocompromise, fixed duration presets, fixed two-to-four-week review, and broad autonomous management remain unsupported.
The workflow must not infer seborrheic dermatitis, infection, psoriasis, or tinea or recommend shampoo, topical medicine, or referral unless clinician-stated.
Qualified clinician review has not been completed.
